verdict_reasoning: HSP60/HSPD1 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but it has extensive documented surface display on tumor cells and stressed cells (cancer ecto-chaperone biology), and is exposed during apoptosis/immunogenic cell death and infection — functioning as a DAMP recognized by innate immune receptors. Multiple groups have detected surface HSP60 by flow/biotinylation on tumor and stressed cells, and antibody approaches targeting cell-surface HSP60 have been described. This is a classic cell-state-induced ecto-chaperone, so contextual rather than no. Not classical surface biology — no TM/GPI anchor of its own.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of HSP60 surface anchoring is unresolved; tumor/stress ecto-pool is well-documented but the size and wash-resistance of the surface fraction varies.
